steps.title: Use the self-critique paradox
steps.content: The underlined sentence says Cruse criticized Black nationalists even though he identified as a Black nationalist. That supports the larger point that he found ways to disagree even with thinkers close to his own position.

steps.title: Reject unsupported functions
steps.content: Choice A says the sentence gives the movement a direction, but it reports criticism. Choice B says his antagonist reputation is undeserved, contradicting the passage. Choice C invents a controversy caused by his work. The sentence clarifies his position among civil rights thinkers. The answer is D.
